purpose: Shinobi — autonomous Windows-native agent for Spanish YouTubers to assist in channel format adaptation.

key_files.name: package.json
key_files.role: Defines dependencies, scripts, and distribution information.

key_files.name: README.md
key_files.role: Provides an overview, setup instructions, and project philosophy.

concerns: README mentions no tests specified in scripts.
License type stated is ISC, while package.json shows MIT.
TODO: Setup commercial and cloud configurations in .env require user input.
No specific test files mentioned for E2E tests in README.
All build and development scripts are present but may need further documentation.

purpose: A continuous watcher for the Hermes repo that polls for new releases and notifies the user.

key_files.name: hermes_watcher.ts
key_files.role: Main functionality for checking updates and managing state.

concerns: No automated tests for most functions.
Possibility of race conditions due to asynchronous state updates.
Error handling is minimal; some failures will leave state unchanged.
Defaults to 24-hour polling, which may not be optimal for all use cases.
State file location is hardcoded to Windows environment, may limit portability.

purpose: Documentation folder containing various reports on the identity, ecosystem, roadmap, and current state of the project.

key_files.name: 00_identity.md
key_files.role: Describes the identity and operational rules for Iván Carbonell.

key_files.name: 01_ecosystem.md
key_files.role: Details the architecture and structure of the ecosystem including directories and technologies.

key_files.name: 02_roadmap.md
key_files.role: Outlines the product roadmap with phases and descriptions of objectives.

key_files.name: 03_state.md
key_files.role: Provides the current status of the project with confirmed functionality and issues.

key_files.name: 04_pending.md
key_files.role: Lists pending technical tasks and issues to be addressed in development.

concerns: Pending tasks include filtering issues in LinkedIn search results for insurance brokers.
Cloudflare Turnstile detection is a problem but currently documented and not blocking core objectives.
No LinkedIn posts have been published yet despite roadmap requirements.
End-to-end agent validation has not been executed in real mission scenarios with new primitives.
Multiple operational guidelines and checks are specified but need human execution.

purpose: Contains scripts for testing and generating reports related to the Shinobi ecosystem.

key_files.name: a3_smoke.ts
key_files.role: Smoke test for parseReadArgs and runRead function.

key_files.name: a4_run.ts
key_files.role: Runs read related tasks and generates reports.

key_files.name: b1_smoke.ts
key_files.role: Smoke test for parseSelfArgs and runSelfDiff.

key_files.name: build_log_generate.mjs
key_files.role: Generates a public build log from commits across multiple repos.

concerns: Ensure tests cover all edge cases for parsing arguments.
Check if reports are correctly formatted before final output.
No explicit tests are mentioned for build_log_generate functionality.
Review permissions for GitHub API access in build log generation.
Validate error handling for file read operations in scripts.

purpose: Contains extracted metadata from a YouTube video related to automated browsing agents.
Contains various demos, skills, and mission-related files for Shinobi automation tasks.

key_files.role: Demo transcript showing execution of representative tasks.

key_files.name: skills/desktop/README.md
key_files.role: Documentation for Shinobi desktop skills for Windows automation.

key_files.name: skills/desktop/desktop-premiere-basic-cut/SKILL.md
key_files.role: Definition and usage instructions for Premiere Pro automation skill.

key_files.name: missions/2026-05-05T21-28-46-431Z_a4_execa/meta.json
key_files.role: Metadata for a specific mission execution.

concerns: TODOs present in skills documentation for verifying application installation.
Some skills cannot be end-to-end tested due to licensing restrictions.
Previous demo transcripts could indicate potential redundancy.
Limited documentation on failures for desktop skills.
Skills require specific software installations, which may complicate usage.
